name: Reply.io
what_it_does: Multi-channel sales engagement platform for automating and personalizing outreach sequences across email, LinkedIn, SMS, WhatsApp, and phone calls with AI-driven reply handling and meeting booking.
use_cases: Running multi-step outreach sequences combining email, LinkedIn, and phone tasks in a single workflow
Automating lead enrollment and sequence triggering from CRM pipeline changes via API
AI-powered reply categorization and automatic follow-up routing based on prospect response sentiment
Building SDR automation workflows where agents enroll prospects, track responses, and escalate positive replies
Meeting booking automation — detecting interested replies and routing to calendar booking links
not_for: Simple single-channel email campaigns where lighter tools like Instantly are more cost-effective
Marketing email to opted-in lists (this is a sales outreach tool)
Small teams without dedicated SDR workflows — multi-channel complexity can be overkill
Developers who need a well-documented, stable API for reliable agent integration
best_when: An enterprise or mid-market sales team runs high-volume SDR operations and needs coordinated multi-channel sequences (email, LinkedIn, phone) managed from a single platform.
avoid_when: You need a simple email-only outreach tool with clean API documentation, or your use case doesn't justify multi-channel complexity.

auth.notes: API key authentication passed as a header parameter (x-api-key). Single key per account, no scope granularity. Keys are managed in the Reply.io dashboard under Settings > API. No OAuth or delegated auth support.

pricing.notes: 14-day free trial. Per-user pricing. LinkedIn automation and AI reply handling require Professional plan. Annual billing discounts available.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Error responses are inconsistent across the API — some endpoints return structured JSON errors while others return plain text messages or HTML error pages. HTTP status code usage is sometimes incorrect (200 returned for errors). No centralized error code reference in documentation.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No idempotency key support. Enrolling the same contact in a sequence twice creates duplicates unless the sequence has duplicate protection enabled at the campaign level.
known_agent_gotchas: Multiple channel types (email, LinkedIn, SMS, WhatsApp, calls) have different setup requirements — agents must verify channel availability before creating steps
LinkedIn steps require a connected LinkedIn account with active session — sessions expire and must be renewed manually, breaking automated sequences
AI reply detection is a platform feature, not an API output — agents cannot query AI reply classifications programmatically on all plans
Contact deduplication across sequences requires checking existing contacts before enrollment — no automatic global deduplication
Sequence steps have type-specific required fields — generic step creation payloads will fail silently if missing channel-specific parameters
API documentation is partially outdated — some documented endpoints have been changed or deprecated without clear changelog
Webhook signature verification is documented but implementation details vary — test thoroughly before relying on webhook auth in production
Rate limits are not clearly published and appear to vary by endpoint and plan tier

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. Single unscoped API key provides full account access. LinkedIn and other channel credentials stored by Reply.io, creating additional credential custody risk. GDPR DPA available. SOC 2 Type II certification claimed. No API key scoping or expiration controls documented.
